mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or the sign of the correlation flips persistently for 6+ months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls by more than 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window after a prior period of elevated unemployment, this signals a repairing labor market and rising future consumption capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.2pp over trailing 3 months while prior level UNRATE > 8%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% in snapshot context represents post-crisis labor market state used to calibrate threshold behavior
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered episodes, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative or the average triggered return is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) reflects broad-based consumer and residential investment demand recovery; when both series rise simultaneously over 3 months, the combined demand impulse produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, exceeding what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change > 0 AND HOUST 3-month change > 0 simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to outperform the single-series RSAFS-only or HOUST-only signal by a statistically significant margin (paired t-test p > 0.05) across walk-forward folds, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns during a post-recession recovery regime because output growth signals improving corporate earnings from a depressed base, but the same rising INDPRO predicts negative or muted returns in a late-cycle regime where it instead signals capacity pressure and impending monetary tightening.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO value 102.28 recorded one month prior to decision date reflects an early post-crisis recovery trajectory
failure_condition: The direction of the INDPRO-return relationship does not flip between early-recovery and late-cycle regime subsamples, or 60d forward returns following INDPRO growth triggers are statistically indistinguishable across regimes (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects real-time business-cycle momentum that is rapidly priced into equities, so positive INDPRO growth is associated with positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as markets extrapolate short-term output momentum.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly INDPRO growth and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 1-year walk-forward windows tested.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI year-over-year inflation crosses above a threshold consistent with rising inflation expectations, markets price in higher probability of near-term monetary tightening, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following CPI-threshold triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the average triggered return is not significantly different from baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a low, stable federal funds rate signals accommodative policy alongside real economic strength, jointly producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; if payroll strength coincides instead with a rising federal funds rate, the tightening offsets the growth benefit and the positive effect disappears.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with labor market expansion
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-growth/low-FEDFUNDS trigger fails to outperform PAYEMS growth alone by a statistically significant margin across walk-forward folds (p > 0.05), indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts predict positive S&P 500 forward returns in an early-cycle recovery regime because they signal reawakening residential investment and consumer confidence, but in a late-cycle regime marked by prior overbuilding, the same rise in housing starts signals oversupply risk and predicts negative or flat short-term returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1354 thousand units two months prior to decision date reflects a low post-crisis base consistent with early recovery
failure_condition: The HOUST-return relationship shows the same sign and magnitude in both early-cycle and late-cycle subsamples, or 5-day forward returns following HOUST increases are not significantly different from baseline in the early-cycle regime (p > 0.05).
